title: Fix CHRONO_MAX_DAYS: u32 silent truncation in ws.rs
description: relay/src/ws.rs contains a const CHRONO_MAX_DAYS: u32 = (i64::MAX / 86400) as u32; i64::MAX/86400 is 106,751,991,167,300 which exceeds u32::MAX (4,294,967,295), so the as u32 cast silently truncates to 579,025,220. The accompanying comment says '~106 million days' but the actual chrono::Duration::days() panic threshold is ~106 trillion days. The guard itself is also redundant: params.duration_days is a u32, so the earlier check params.duration_days as u64 > max_days (max_days = operator's SUBSCRIPTION_DAYS, default 30) always fires first; no realistic subscription length can reach 579M days let alone 4.29B. Fix: remove the redundant CHRONO_MAX_DAYS guard entirely, or if kept, correct the type to u64 and the value to 106_751_991 (matching the correct constant in main.rs) and fix the comment.
